sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Targets stuck at cols 12-27 (attached) + Red at 42-45 (detached). Path reached minimum length, can't retract further. Need different approach.
Plan: Extend path RIGHT to position targets, then use DOWN to detach them one by one. Try RIGHT x2 to move attached targets right.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Extend path RIGHT to move attached targets right
# Current: Green at 12-15, Blue at 18-21, Orange at 24-27
# After 2 RIGHT: Green at 24-27, Blue at 30-33, Orange at 36-39
# Red is detached at 42-45

action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("After 2 RIGHT:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['N', 'b', 'O', 'R'] and n['pixels'] >= 4:
        min_row = min(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])
        if min_row < 54:
            rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
            cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
            print(f"  {n['color']}: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Check if Red reattached
targets = {}
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['N', 'b', 'O', 'R'] and n['pixels'] >= 4:
        min_row = min(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])
        if min_row < 54:
            targets[n['color']] = True
print(f"\nTargets in room: {set(targets.keys())}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

  O: rows 25-28, cols 36-39
  R: rows 25-28, cols 42-45

Targets in room: {'b', 'O', 'R', 'N'}

sections.content:

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Extend path RIGHT to move attached targets right
# Current: Green at 12-15, Blue at 18-21, Orange at 24-27
# After 2 RIGHT: Green at 24-27, Blue at 30-33, Orange at 36-39
# Red is detached at 42-45

action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("After 2 RIGHT:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['N', 'b', 'O', 'R'] and n['pixels'] >= 4:
        min_row = min(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])
        if min_row < 54:
            rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
            cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
            print(f"  {n['color']}: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Check if Red reattached
targets = {}
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['N', 'b', 'O', 'R'] and n['pixels'] >= 4:
        min_row = min(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])
        if min_row < 54:
            targets[n['color']] = True
print(f"\nTargets in room: {set(targets.keys())}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
